content.problemStatement:
Given NNN pairs of parentheses. Find all **valid parentheses** string of length 2⋅N2 \cdot N2⋅N.

Note: A **valid parentheses** string is defined as:


Empty string is valid.
If PPP is valid, (P)(P)(P) is also valid.
If PPP and QQQ are valid, PQPQPQ is also valid.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input, containing integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output M+1M+1M+1 lines, where MMM is the number of valid parentheses:



The first line contains a single integer MMM.
The next MMM lines contain string of length 2⋅N2 \cdot N2⋅N.

Note: The valid parentheses strings must be printed in **lexicographically increasing** order.
String SSS is said to be lexicographically smaller than string TTT if there exists a position iii where Si<TiS_i \lt T_iSi​<Ti​ and Sj=TjS_j = T_jSj​=Tj​ for all j<ij \lt ij<i.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There will be 222 valid parenthesis strings (())(())(()), ()()()()()().
Test case 222: There will be 555 valid parenthesis strings ((()))((()))((())), (()())(()())(()()), (())()(())()(())(), ()(())()(())()(()), ()()()()()()()()().
